World.title: Hungarians vote on whether to end 16 years of Orbán rule and elect rival
World.desc: Most polls favour Péter Magyar, who fronts a grassroots party, and early figures suggest a big turnout of voters.

World.title: 'We need real peace': Easter truce fails to lift grim mood in war-torn Ukraine
World.desc: Easter festivities are muted in Kharkiv as Ukrainians expect fighting to flare up again after a weekend truce.

World.title: At least 30 feared dead in crush at Haitian tourist site
World.desc: The incident occurred at a "tourist event attended by many young people", Haiti's prime minister says.

World.title: Prosecutor probes abuse claims against California governor candidate Swalwell
World.desc: The high-profile Democrat, a leading contender in California's governor race, firmly denies the accusations.

World.title: Why this disillusioned Trump voter spends hours searching Epstein files
World.desc: Many who support the president have expressed frustration with how Trump's former attorney general Pam Bondi handled the Epstein files.

World.title: Germany's far-right AfD adopts 'radical' manifesto ahead of key polls
World.desc: The far-right party is riding high in the opinion polls in the eastern state of Saxony-Anhalt.

Technology.title: Dancer with MND performs on stage again through digital avatar
Technology.desc: Breanna Olson said brainwave tech was able to re-establish the expression and connection she felt had gone.

Technology.title: Meta pulls Facebook ads recruiting for social media addiction lawsuits
Technology.desc: The Facebook owner recently lost a landmark social media addiction trial in California

Technology.title: White House staff told not to place bets on prediction markets
Technology.desc: The gambling platforms have grown in popularity, with some users making wagers on conflicts.

Technology.title: Mayor warns of London 'disinformation blizzard'
Technology.desc: Sir Sadiq Khan claims London is being targeted with disinformation portraying it as a city "in decline".

Technology.title: Amazon to end support for older Kindles, prompting user outcry
Technology.desc: The move means owners of Kindles released before 2013 will be unable to download new e-books.

Technology.title: OpenAI pauses UK data centre deal over energy costs and regulation
Technology.desc: The project was part of a package of tech investment promising the UK could become an AI superpower.

Science.title: Back to Earth: What happens to the Artemis II astronauts now?
Science.desc: The astronauts will have medical checks and will be reunited with their families.

Science.title: Artemis II mission was a triumph. Now comes the hard part
Science.desc: The mission was almost flawless but there are considerable obstacles ahead before a Moon landing.

Science.title: Want to help garden birds? Don't feed them in warmer months, says RSPB
Science.desc: The UK's largest bird charity has issued new guidance advising people to stop using feeders to help wildlife thrive.

Science.title: Pioneering wildlife cameraman Doug Allan dies after falling ill in Nepal
Science.desc: The cameraman and photographer won eight Emmy Awards for his work on acclaimed series like Blue Planet alongside Sir David Attenborough.

Science.title: Has Artemis II shown we can land on the Moon again?
Science.desc: The Artemis II mission has been near flawless to date, but has the test flight shown Nasa is ready to send humans to the lunar surface?

Science.title: Earthset and a solar eclipse: Nasa releases first images from Moon fly-by
Science.desc: The first two images show an 'Earthrise' and the solar eclipse the astronauts viewed.
